description: 67-category annotation framework for the Canadian Climate Framing dataset.

definitions.economic_frame: Sentence refers to climate change as an economic issue (impacts on the economy, costs/benefits of climate action, carbon footprint of sectors).
definitions.eco_neg_impact: Negative effects of climate change on the economy (infrastructure damage, agricultural loss, economic decline).
definitions.eco_pos_impact: Positive effects of climate change on the economy (new agricultural opportunities, new trade routes).
definitions.eco_cost: Negative economic impacts of climate policies (transition costs, job losses in fossil fuels).
definitions.eco_benefit: Positive economic impacts of climate policies (renewable jobs, green-tech investment).
definitions.eco_footprint: Carbon footprint of economic/industrial sectors (manufacturing, transport, energy, mining, agriculture).
definitions.health_frame: Climate change as a public health issue (health impacts, co-benefits, healthcare carbon footprint).
definitions.health_neg_impact: Negative health effects of climate change (infectious/respiratory disease, heat illness, mental health).
definitions.health_pos_impact: [EXCLUDED — insufficient training data] Positive health effects of climate change.
definitions.health_cobenefit: Health benefits from climate policies (clean air, better nutrition, fewer respiratory diseases).
definitions.health_footprint: [EXCLUDED — insufficient training data] Carbon footprint of health/medical sector.
definitions.security_frame: Climate change as a security issue (conflict, refugees, military disruption, defense footprint).
definitions.security_conflict: Conflicts over natural resources due to climate change (water, land, energy).
definitions.security_disruption: [PARTIAL] Disruption of military operations by climate-related disasters.
definitions.justice_frame: Climate change as a justice/moral issue (winners/losers, responsibility, vulnerability, intergenerational).
definitions.justice_winners: Climate policies create winners and losers, benefiting some at the expense of others.
definitions.justice_responsibility: Unequal responsibility for climate change (CBDR principle).
definitions.justice_vulnerability: Unequal exposure to climate impacts (geographic, social, economic vulnerability).
definitions.justice_access: Unequal capacity to act on climate (financial, technical, institutional).
definitions.justice_intergen: Intergenerational justice — current decisions and future generations' rights.
definitions.political_frame: Climate change as a political issue (policy, debates, party positions, public opinion data).
definitions.pol_action: Formal approval / enactment of climate policies.
definitions.pol_debate: Political debates about climate policies, effectiveness, government responsibility.
definitions.pol_position: Explicit stances by politicians or parties on climate policies.
definitions.pol_opinion: Surveys, polls, studies measuring public attitudes toward climate.
definitions.scientific_frame: Climate change as a scientific issue (research, discoveries, consensus, controversy).
definitions.sci_debate: Debates within the scientific community about climate science or technologies.
definitions.sci_discovery: Climate science mechanisms or new scientific discoveries.
definitions.sci_skepticism: Questioning the validity, accuracy, or sufficiency of climate science.
definitions.sci_defense: Defending the robustness/credibility of climate science against skepticism.
definitions.environmental_frame: Climate change as environmental issue (habitat, biodiversity, fauna/flora).
definitions.env_species: Negative impacts on animal/plant species — habitat changes, mortality, biodiversity loss.
definitions.cultural_frame: Climate change as a cultural issue (art, events, Indigenous practices, sector footprint).
definitions.cult_art: Cultural representations of climate change (art, literature, music, film).
definitions.cult_event_impact: Climate disrupting cultural or sports events.
definitions.cult_indigenous: Erosion of Indigenous cultural practices due to climate change.
definitions.cult_footprint: Carbon footprint of cultural / artistic / sports sectors.
definitions.msg_health: Quoted statement from someone with medical/health expertise.
definitions.msg_economic: Quoted statement from someone with economic/financial expertise.
definitions.msg_security: Quoted statement from someone with security/defense expertise.
definitions.msg_legal: Quoted statement from someone with legal expertise.
definitions.msg_cultural: Quoted statement from someone with cultural/artistic/sports expertise.
definitions.msg_scientist: Quoted statement from someone with natural-science expertise.
definitions.msg_social: Quoted statement from someone with social-science expertise.
definitions.msg_activist: Quoted statement from an activist or advocacy organization.
definitions.msg_official: Quoted statement from a politician or government representative.
definitions.event: Sentence refers to an event (weather, political, meeting, publication, cultural, protest).
definitions.evt_weather: Extreme meteorological events causing disruption or harm.
definitions.evt_publication: Release of reports, studies, or articles.
definitions.solution: Sentence refers to climate solutions (mitigation or adaptation).
definitions.sol_adaptation: Strategies to manage climate impacts and enhance resilience.
definitions.tone_positive: Tone is optimistic, reassuring, or enthusiastic.
definitions.tone_negative: Tone is alarming, critical, or pessimistic.
definitions.tone_neutral: Tone is informative or descriptive without strong emotion.
definitions.canada: Sentence refers to Canada in any context.
definitions.urgency: Sentence conveys urgency or alarmism about climate action.
definitions.ner_entities: JSON object with keys PER (persons), ORG (organizations), LOC (locations).
